bull.b: Very high gross margins (~88% TTM) combined with multi-year revenue step-up and Q1 €247M sales indicate attractive unit economics and scalable product pricing. Durable margins provide structural room to absorb SG&A/R&D as commercialization scales and support long-term operating‑margin improvement if volume growth persi

Operating and free cash flow have turned positive (TTM), and Q1 cash of €573M plus monetization of a PRV ($187.5M) and redemption of convertible notes materially boost near‑term liquidity. This strengthens funding flexibility for commercialization and trials and reduces reliance on dilutive financings over the medium t

Rapid uptake of YORVIPATH (€197M Q1; >1,000 new U.S. patients) and FDA approval plus immediate prescriptions for YUVIWEL demonstrate durable commercial execution, expanding prescriber base and payer access. These product-level traction points underpin predictable recurring revenue streams and support economies of scale

bear.t: Meaningful Leverage And Past Negative Equity
bear.b: A sizeable debt load (~$0.9B) and history of negative equity constrain financial flexibility. If operating margins fail to sustainably improve, debt servicing and covenant pressures could force dilutive financings or cutbacks in R&D/commercial investment, impeding long‑term growth prospects despite recent cash inflows.

The large GAAP profit is materially explained by a one‑time deferred tax asset; underlying operations still show operating losses historically and modest operating cash conversion. Reliance on accounting one‑offs and non‑recurring items raises sustainability questions about reported profitability and cash‑backed earnin

Ongoing ITC/legal matters and supply/capacity timing for YUVIWEL create persistent execution risk that could delay shipments, constrain uptake, or increase costs. Such structural regulatory and manufacturing uncertainties can slow revenue ramp and erode payer/provider confidence over multiple quarters.
